mentions.note: Beat Q1 and raised guidance; Brian Niccol's service-first turnaround driving loyalty. Gross margins now 10%, bouncing to 13% soon; stock becomes cheap once margins normalize. Long runway.
Stock creeping higher; level has been challenging but looks ready to break out.

Big position in Travel Trust; challenges: inability to close underperforming stores and reallocate capital, geographic imbalance (overweight coasts, underweight Midwest); CEO Brian Nickel working on it but takes time.

Throughput turnaround under CEO Brian Nickel working; same-store sales beat; first growth in both rewards and non-rewards transactions in eight quarters. Menu simplification, consistent service, bigger rosters drive durability; Cramer holding large position; bullish long-term if you're not yet in.
Wildly overbought; reports Wednesday with investor day Thursday. Stock can't keep climbing unless numbers insanely strong; like long-term but doubt it justifies current ramp.
Rallied to $93.28 from $70s on Brian Niccol turnaround execution; slow inch-by-inch recovery resuming; Cramer backing turnaround despite Seven Brews competition.

Turnaround candidate under new CEO Brian Nickel (ex-Chipotle) fixing execution, throughput, and closing underperforming stores; Cramer thinks 2026 is comeback year but management transition still proving.
